channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump says 'consequential' presidents face more danger after WHCA dinner shooting
item.description: After the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner in Washington, D.C., the White House was quick to praise law enforcement and call for unity, controlling the narrative early.
item.content:encoded: After the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner in Washington, D.C., the White House was quick to praise law enforcement and call for unity, controlling the narrative early.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Rep. Jamie Raskin shares his experience from the White House Correspondents' dinner
item.description: Steve Inskeep asks Maryland Democrat Jamie Raskin, ranking member of the House Judiciary Committee with oversight of the Secret Service, about experiencing Saturday's correspondents' dinner shooting.
item.content:encoded: Steve Inskeep asks Maryland Democrat Jamie Raskin, ranking member of the House Judiciary Committee with oversight of the Secret Service, about experiencing Saturday's correspondents' dinner shooting.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: East Africa redefines marathon limits as Sabastian Sawe leads historic charge
item.description: East Africa has rewritten marathon history as Sabastian Sawe produced a stunning breakthrough at the London Marathon, redefining what was thought possible over the marathon distance.
item.content:encoded:
East Africa has rewritten marathon history as Sabastian Sawe produced a stunning breakthrough at the London Marathon, redefining what was thought possible over the marathon distance.

(Image credit: Alberto Pezzali)
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Igunza

item.title: Musk vs. Altman: Tech CEOs head to court over the fate of OpenAI
item.description: The former OpenAI business partners are embroiled in a high-stakes dispute over the future of one of the world's top AI companies.
item.content:encoded:
The former OpenAI business partners are embroiled in a high-stakes dispute over the future of one of the world's top AI companies.

(Image credit: Michael Kovac/Getty Images for Vanity Fair)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Ingenious? Orwellian? Or both? Supreme Court considers constitutionality of 'geofence' warrants
item.description: The technique allows police to tap into giant tech-firm databases to find out who was near the scene of a crime and may have been involved.
item.content:encoded: The technique allows police to tap into giant tech-firm databases to find out who was near the scene of a crime and may have been involved.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Scientists search the microbiome for clues to the rise in colorectal cancers
item.description: Unlike many cancers, colorectal cancer has become more lethal for people at younger ages. Doctors are sleuthing out why.
item.content:encoded: Unlike many cancers, colorectal cancer has become more lethal for people at younger ages. Doctors are sleuthing out why.
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: White House responds to correspondents' dinner shooting with praise and blame, investigation into the shooting and suspect continues, King Charles arrives in U.S. for state visit.
item.content:encoded: White House responds to correspondents' dinner shooting with praise and blame, investigation into the shooting and suspect continues, King Charles arrives in U.S. for state visit.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Planet Money explores how economics shape the world in new book
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Alex Mayyasi, the author of Planet Money's new book, "Planet Money: A Guide to the Economic Forces That Shape Your Life."
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Alex Mayyasi, the author of Planet Money's new book, "Planet Money: A Guide to the Economic Forces That Shape Your Life."
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Congress under pressure to end DHS shutdown as it returns to session this week
item.description: Congress returns this week with added pressure to find a deal to end the partial DHS shutdown. It also needs to find a way to end the impasse over FISA surveillance legislation.
item.content:encoded: Congress returns this week with added pressure to find a deal to end the partial DHS shutdown. It also needs to find a way to end the impasse over FISA surveillance legislation.
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: Michel Martin on shooting at White House Correspondents' Association dinner
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks co-host Michel Martin about her experience as shots were fired at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks co-host Michel Martin about her experience as shots were fired at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
